description: §19.9.8 — scrml has no `async` keyword. The DEFAULT parse path (block-splitter + ast-builder) fires the hard rejection E-ASYNC-NOT-IN-SCRML on a user-source `async function`, matching the opt-in `--parser=scrml-native` path (parse-stmt.js). Migration 2026-07: this REVERSES the retired S89 Q5 `I-ASYNC-USER-SOURCE` info nudge (which let user async compile); `notCodes` asserts the nudge is gone.
rationale: §19.9.8 (user-voice S114: 'I hate leaky abstractions and colored functions'): scrml has no `async`/`await` — the canonical async surface is the compiler body-split / CPS (§19.9.3). A source-level `async function`/`async fn`/`async () =>` is a hard rejection: E-ASYNC-NOT-IN-SCRML. The `async` modifier is reserved for `scrml:*` stdlib declarations (§13.1 carve-out — Promise<T> signal to the auto-await classifier).
